onboarding.tourDescription: Let us walk you through the key features of Clouisle
onboarding.tourOverviewDescription: Learn about the home page and workspace navigation
onboarding.tourModelsDescription: Learn about available AI models and configuration
onboarding.tourKBDescription: Learn how to create and manage Knowledge Bases
onboarding.tourAppCreateDescription: Create your first Agent or Workflow
onboarding.tourAppConfigDescription: Configure prompts, knowledge bases, tools, and publish your Agent

step1.title: Welcome to Clouisle
step1.description: This is your workspace navigation. Use these links to access different sections: Home, Apps, Knowledge Bases, Capabilities, and Models.

step1b.title: Workspace Overview
step1b.description: This is the workspace home page, where you can quickly understand recent team usage, resource distribution, and common entry points.

step2.title: Dashboard Overview
step2.description: Here you can see key metrics for your team: conversations, messages, tokens used, and success rate.

step2b.title: Usage Trends
step2b.description: This chart shows recent conversation and token usage trends, helping you understand team growth, activity changes, and resource consumption.

step2c.title: Resource Overview
step2c.description: This chart shows how many resources your team has by type, including Agents, Workflows, Knowledge Bases, and Models.

step3.title: Quick Actions
step3.description: Use these shortcuts to quickly create Agents, Workflows, Knowledge Bases, or manage Tools.

step3b.title: Recent Items
step3b.description: This area shows recently created or updated apps and resources, so you can quickly return to what you are configuring or using.

step4.title: Model Configuration
step4.description: Click here to view available AI models. Your administrator configures which models are available for your team.

step5.title: Available Models
step5.description: Here you can see the models available for your team. Click on a model card to view detailed quota information.

step6.title: Knowledge Bases
step6.description: Click here to manage your Knowledge Bases. Knowledge Bases allow your Agents to access your documents and data.

step7.title: Create Knowledge Base
step7.description: Click this card to create your first Knowledge Base. You can upload documents, web pages, or connect data sources.

step7b.title: Import Knowledge Base
step7b.description: You can also import an existing Knowledge Base from a package file. Click this button to import.

step8.title: Apps Section
step8.description: Click here to manage your Agents and Workflows. This is where you build AI applications.

step9.title: Create Your First Agent
step9.description: Click this button to open the Agent creation dialog. After creating your Agent, you'll be taken to its configuration page where you can set up prompts, knowledge bases, and more.

step9b.title: Select App Type
step9b.description: Choose the type of app you want to create: Agent for conversational scenarios, Workflow for process orchestration. Click either one to continue.

step9c.title: Enter Name
step9c.description: Enter a name for your app. The name is required. Click "Next" when you're ready to continue.

step9d.title: Description (Optional)
step9d.description: You can add a description for your app. This is optional. Click "Next" when you're ready to continue.

step9e.title: Create App
step9e.description: You're all set! Click this button to create your app.

step11.title: Configure Prompt
step11.description: This is the system prompt where you define your Agent's behavior. Describe what your Agent should do, its personality, and any rules it should follow. You can also use the "AI Generate" button for assistance.

step11b.title: AI Generate Prompt
step11b.description: Click this button to let AI automatically generate a prompt based on your description. You just provide a simple description, and AI will generate a detailed prompt.

step11c.title: Variables
step11c.description: Variables allow you to define placeholders in your prompt that users can fill in when using the Agent. For example, you can define a variable called 'username'.

step12.title: Link Knowledge Base
step12.description: Optionally link a Knowledge Base to give your Agent access to your documents and data. The Agent will use this information to answer questions.

step13.title: Add Tools
step13.description: Optionally add tools to extend your Agent's capabilities. Tools allow your Agent to perform actions like web searches, calculations, or API calls.

step14.title: Save Configuration
step14.description: Click here to save your Agent configuration. Remember to save after making changes!

step14a.title: Image Understanding
step14a.description: When enabled, your Agent can understand and analyze image content. Useful for scenarios that require processing images or extracting information from pictures.

step14b.title: File Upload
step14b.description: When enabled, users can upload files to your Agent. Supports PDF, Word, Excel, and other formats. The Agent can read file content to answer questions.

step14c.title: User Input Request
step14c.description: When enabled, your Agent can proactively request additional information from users during conversations. The Agent will ask you when it needs more details to complete a task.

step14d.title: Memory
step14d.description: When enabled, your Agent remembers important information from conversations and maintains context across sessions. Ideal for personal assistants or customer service scenarios requiring long-term memory.

step14e.title: Image Generation
step14e.description: When enabled, your Agent can generate images from text descriptions. Suitable for creative design, content production, and other scenarios requiring visual content generation.

step14f.title: Video Generation
step14f.description: When enabled, your Agent can generate videos from text descriptions. Suitable for marketing, tutorial creation, and other scenarios requiring video content generation.

step14g.title: Embed Configuration
step14g.description: Click this button to open the embed configuration dialog. Here you can generate embed code to integrate your Agent into your website or application. You can choose from three embed modes: Bubble (floating chat button), Fullscreen (full-page embed), or Mobile (mobile-optimized). You can also customize the theme, colors, and bubble position. Once configured, simply copy the generated code and paste it into your website's HTML to embed your Agent.

step14l.title: Agent Settings
step14l.description: Click this button to open the Agent settings drawer. Here you can configure basic information, model settings, and conversation settings for your Agent.

step14m.title: Agent Icon
step14m.description: Upload an icon for your Agent. This icon will be displayed in the conversation interface and app list. You can click to upload an image or select from available icons.

step14n.title: Agent Name
step14n.description: Set the name of your Agent. The name will be displayed in the conversation interface and app list. Choose a clear and descriptive name.

step14o.title: Agent Description
step14o.description: Add a description for your Agent. The description helps users understand what your Agent does and what it's suitable for. This is optional but recommended.

step14p.title: Visibility
step14p.description: Set the visibility of your Agent: Private means only you can see and use it; Team means all team members can see and use it. Choose based on your needs.

step14q.title: Model Selection
step14q.description: Select the AI model for your Agent. Different models have different capabilities and performance. You can switch models here based on your needs.

step14r.title: Max Iterations
step14r.description: Set the maximum number of iterations for the Agent. This controls how many rounds the Agent can execute in a single conversation. Higher values allow more complex tasks but consume more tokens.

step14s.title: Hide Tool Calls
step14s.description: When enabled, the Agent's tool call process will be hidden from the conversation interface. Suitable for scenarios where you don't want users to see the technical details of tool usage.

step14t.title: Conversation Settings
step14t.description: This is the conversation settings section. Click to expand and collapse this section. Here you can configure the opening message and suggested questions for your Agent.

step14u.title: Opening Message
step14u.description: Set the opening message for your Agent. This message will be displayed when the user first opens the conversation, helping users understand how to use the Agent.

step14v.title: Suggested Questions
step14v.description: Set suggested questions for your Agent. These questions will appear as quick options at the beginning of the conversation, helping users get started faster. Enter one question per line.

step14w.title: Close Settings
step14w.description: Click this button to close the settings drawer and return to the Agent configuration page. Your settings will be automatically saved.

step15.title: Save Configuration
step15.description: Click here to save your Agent configuration. Remember to save after making changes!

step15b.title: Configuration Saved
step15b.description: After saving, your Agent is ready for publishing. Next, you can publish it so it can be officially accessed and used.

step16.title: Publish Agent
step16.description: Click this button to open the publish confirmation menu. Once published, your Agent will be available for use. You can also unpublish it later if needed.

step16b.title: Confirm Publish
step16b.description: Click confirm publish to make this Agent active and available to users with access.

step16c.title: Published Successfully
step16c.description: After publishing, you can use the Agent from the more professional chat page, or integrate it into business scenarios through embed or API access.

step17.title: Your Agent Card
step17.description: This is your Agent card. It shows the Agent's name, description, and status. Hover over the card and click the three-dot menu to access more actions like chatting with your Agent, publishing/unpublishing, or managing settings.

step17a.title: More Actions
step17a.description: Click the three-dot menu to open more actions for this Agent.

step17b.title: Chat with Agent
step17b.description: Click the chat button to open a more professional and user-friendly chat page, where you can use and interact with this Agent in a focused experience.

step18.title: Enter Configuration
step18.description: Click this Agent card to enter the configuration page. Here you can set up prompts, link knowledge bases, add tools, and configure all aspects of your Agent.

step10a.title: Orchestration
step10a.description: This is the Agent's orchestration configuration page. Here you can set up prompts, knowledge bases, tools, and other core features.

step10b.title: API
step10b.description: View your Agent's API information. Here you can get API keys, endpoint addresses, and learn how to call your Agent through code.

step10c.title: Logs
step10c.description: View your Agent's run logs. Records all conversation history and Agent execution processes for debugging and analysis.

step10d.title: Monitor
step10d.description: Monitor your Agent's runtime status. View key metrics: conversations, success rate, token usage, etc., to understand Agent performance.

step10.title: Chat with Your Agent
step10.description: This is the chat interface. Type your message here and press Enter or click Send to interact with your Agent.
